content: Just as MemGPT uses hierarchical virtual context management to expand an LLM's effective context window beyond its fixed physical context limit like an OS, we can reframe biological and chemical agents as naturally evolved hierarchical context managers that dynamically swap active environmental stimulus data in and out of their limited working "response capacity" to act efficiently across far larger and more complex problem spaces than their immediate processing can hold. This framing suggests all agents, biological or artificial, rely on tiered memory management to sustain effective long-range action.
